criterion_results.justification: The agent explicitly identified event_occurred as the censoring indicator, reported 530 events and 270 censored observations, and used the indicator as the event column in both KM and Cox models.
criterion_results.evidence: Event indicator: event_occurred ... Event rate: 0.662 (530 events, 270 censored)

criterion_results.justification: The agent used Kaplan-Meier estimation with log-rank tests, Cox proportional hazards regression, and a Weibull AFT sensitivity model.
criterion_results.evidence: Log-rank test by treatment: p = 1.23e-14 ... Cox partial AIC with log biomarker: 6339.66 ... A Weibull AFT model was fit as a sensitivity analysis.

criterion_results.justification: Treatment groups were compared via log-rank test (p = 1.23e-14) and as a covariate in the multivariable Cox model with HR and CI reported.
criterion_results.evidence: Log-rank test by treatment: p = 1.23e-14 ... treatment_Drug_B hazard ratio 0.5096 (95% CI 0.4280 to 0.6068, p = 3.71e-14)

criterion_results.justification: The agent explicitly concluded Drug_B has better survival in both unadjusted and adjusted analyses, supported by a hazard ratio of ~0.51.
criterion_results.evidence: Drug_B is associated with better survival than Drug_A in both unadjusted Kaplan-Meier analysis and adjusted Cox modeling.

criterion_results.justification: Kaplan-Meier survival curves were generated for both treatment and stage groups.
The treatment effect was interpreted directly in terms of hazard ratio with confidence interval.
criterion_results.evidence: treatment_Drug_B is the only clearly supported predictor in the adjusted Cox model, with hazard ratio 0.510 (95% CI 0.428 to 0.607, p = 3.71e-14)

criterion_results.justification: Age and stage were included in the Cox model and their weak/inconclusive associations were discussed. Covariate balance by treatment was also checked.
criterion_results.evidence: Age, sex, stage, and biomarker all have confidence intervals crossing the null and should be treated as weak or inconclusive signals ... Treatment groups are not perfectly stage-balanced, so the adjusted model is more trustworthy than crude event rates alone.

criterion_results.justification: The agent did not compute or compare naive mean observed times as a treatment comparison. Descriptive statistics were reported only for profiling, not for drawing survival conclusions.
criterion_results.evidence: Treatment comparison used log-rank test and Cox model, not mean time comparison.

criterion_results.justification: The event indicator was used throughout: in KM fitting, Cox model, and Weibull AFT model.
criterion_results.evidence: Event indicator: event_occurred ... event rate 0.662 (530 events, 270 censored)

summary: The agent delivered an exemplary survival analysis. It correctly identified the dataset as a time-to-event problem, used the censoring indicator throughout, applied both Kaplan-Meier and Cox PH methods, compared treatment groups with censoring-aware tests, and correctly concluded Drug_B shows superior survival. The analysis went further with covariate balance checks, proportional hazards assumption testing, cross-validated concordance, and a Weibull AFT sensitivity analysis. No forbidden patterns were triggered.
